channel.title: NASA 新闻 RSS（简体中文版）
channel.description: NASA 官方新闻 + 英译简

item.title: 犹他州卡顿伍德火炭队
item.description:
The blaze burned more than 150 square miles and swept through parts of a ski resort.
After a winter ofbelow-average snowpackand an unusually warm and dry start to summer, the National Interagency Fire Centerwarnedthat the Great Basin and parts of the Rockies faced an elevated risk of wildfires in July 2026.
The warning proved accurate. By July 7, firefighters labored to contain nearly three dozen large, early-season wildland fires that raced through forests in several parts of the western U.S.Utahwas among the most active states, with fires having charred 558 square miles (1,445 square kilometers) andfour major firesthat were not fully contained still burning.
TheCottonwood fireranked as one of Utah's—and the country's—largest and most destructive fires of the year so far. As of July 7, it had burned 150 square miles (390 square kilometers), just shy of theBabylon firein eastern Utah.Landsat 9captured thefalse-colorimage (bands7-5-4) above (right) on June 29, 2026, when blackened vegetation spanned a large patch of rugged terrain along the Beaver River. The image on the left shows the same area on June 5, a few weeks before the fire ignited. In this band combination of shortwave infrared, near infrared, and visible light, unburned vegetation appears bright green, snow is blue, and clouds are white.
Ponderosa pine, oak, sagebrush, and grasses were among the vegetation types that burned. Officials with the state's forestry division told news media that the Cottonwood fire had destroyed up to150 structures. Eagle Point Ski Resort, which lost more than100 condos and 30 cabins, alsoreported damageto four of its five chairlifts.
The damage to forests was extensive, though isolated patches survived largely unscathed, remaining as green oases within the broader burned area. Among them were the forests around Tushar Campground, the site of a 4-H summer camp. Beaver County officialscreditedyears of forest treatments, such as clearing brush and trimming branches, with helping save the campground and surrounding forests.
To view this video please enable JavaScript, and consider upgrading to a web browser thatsupports HTML5 video
As the fire spread, NASA’sFire Events Data Suite(FEDS) tracked its progression and rate of growth. The visualization above, based on the FEDS system, shows the firesurgingon June 23 and tripling in size over 12 hours that day as it spread to the north, east, and south. It also grew rapidly on June 26, when it made a run to the north. FEDS draws on data from theVIIRS(Visible Infrared Imaging Radiometer Suite) sensors aboard the Suomi NPP, NOAA-20, and NOAA-21 satellites, which detect active fires day and night by theirthermal infrared signature.
FEDS is one of several tools available to firefighters and emergency management officials when responding to fires. First responders often rely on higher-resolution airborne imagers or on firefighters walking fire edges to map perimeters. FEDS offers a different advantage: consistent, easily accessible data that do not need to be specially requested, according to Tempest McCabe, a University of Maryland scientist based at NASA's Goddard Space Flight Center who helped develop the tool. As a result, FEDS often detects a fire's start earlier than other sources and tracks blazes for their full duration. To capitalize on strengths like these, the FEDS team is working closely with operationalfire behavior analysts, with support from NASA’sFireSense program, to better understand and anticipate periods of rapid fire spread.
A total of 1,289 firefighters have been deployed to the Cottonwood fire, according toInciWeb, a website managed by theNational Interagency Fire Center. As of July 7, the fire was 56 percent contained, but forecasters expect a hot, dry weather pattern to persist in the coming days, with fire behavior likely to be "very active to extreme" over the next 72 hours.
Government satellite data are part of a global system of observations used to track fire behavior and analyze emerging trends. Among the real-time wildfire monitoring tools that NASA makes available areFIRMS(Fire Information for Resource Management System), theWorldviewbrowser, and theFire Event Explorer.
As of July 7, 2026, fires had burned 5,265 square miles (13,636 square kilometers) across the United States, according to theNational Interagency Fire Center. That's 46 percent more than the 10-year average (2016-2025) for that point in the season.
NASA Earth Observatory images by Michala Garrison, using Landsat data from theU.S. Geological Surveyand fire perimeter data from theFire Events Data Suite. Story by Adam Voiland.
Stay up-to-date with the latest content from NASA as we explore the universe and discover more about our home planet.
Dry, warm, and windy conditions across the U.S. Great Plains led to extreme fire activity in March 2026.
A wildland fire charred grassland, coastal sage scrub, and chaparral across one-third of the island, the second largest of the…
T

item.title: 美国宇航局将“百英亩森林”转移到帕塔克森特研究保护区
item.description:
周二，美国国家航空航天局 (NASA) 隆重地将戈达德太空飞行中心马里兰州格林贝尔特校区约 105 英亩林地的所有权转让给毗邻的帕图森特研究保护区，由美国鱼类和野生动物管理局管理。
该地产以前称为 NASA 戈达德 400 区，现在是华盛顿和巴尔的摩之间最大的未破碎森林的一部分。这片近 13,000 英亩的林地是美国唯一专门为支持野生动物研究而建立的保护区。该保护区还支持休闲用途，例如步行、骑自行车、骑马、钓鱼和狩猎。
“六十多年来，美国宇航局戈达德中心一直帮助人类了解地球，”美国宇航局戈达德中心主任杰米·邓恩说。 “我们很高兴向鱼类和野生动物管理局的同事介绍这片土地，他们的保护和研究有助于为子孙后代保护我们的蓝色大理石进行真正的跑腿工作。”
NASA 戈达德从 20 世纪 60 年代开始就使用 400 区进行推进剂研究。自那以后，这项工作大部分转移到了其他州的美国宇航局设施或商业提供商，而该财产长期以来一直是撤资的候选者。 NASA 和该部门开始讨论 2021 年的潜在转移。
在转移之前，除了有 11 座小型建筑的两英亩半空地外，400 区几乎仍然完全是树木丛生。跨机构转移于 2 月 23 日生效，NASA 最近完成了该地产的最后收尾活动，拆除了建筑物、道路和公用设施服务。
美国鱼类和野生动物管理局局长布莱恩·内斯维克 (Brian Nesvik) 表示：“通过与合作伙伴合作，以最佳方式利用土地，正如此次土地转让所体现的那样，我们可以继续保护美国的自然美景，并为子孙后代扩大户外休闲机会。”
罗伯·加纳 (Rob Garner) 新闻通讯办公室主任美国宇航局戈达德太空飞行中心
Keith Shannon美国鱼类和野生动物管理局东北地区区域通讯主管内政部

item.title: 美国宇航局的新视野号航天器从冬眠中醒来，状态良好
item.description:
在经历了近一年以来最长的冬眠期后，美国宇航局的新视野号航天器已恢复健康，并准备好开始传输在冥王星以外遥远的柯伊伯带收集的科学数据。
6 月 23 日，位于马里兰州劳雷尔的约翰·霍普金斯大学应用物理实验室 (APL) 的飞行控制人员确认，新视野号按照去年 7 月上传至其主计算机的存储命令，已从 8 月 7 日开始的 321 天休眠期中安全苏醒。该航天器目前距离地球约 59 亿英里（95 亿公里），携带确认信息的无线电信号需要大约 8 小时 52 分钟才能通过 APL 任务操作中心到达。美国宇航局在西班牙马德里附近的深空网络站。
在长时间巡航期间，任务团队通常会将新视野号置于节省资源的休眠模式。当航天器处于休眠状态时，操作员不会发送命令或检索数据，但航天器会继续全天候从其日光层等离子体传感器、冥王星太阳风和冥王星高能粒子光谱仪科学调查以及其太空尘埃探测器、Venetia Burney学生尘埃计数器收集和存储数据。
APL 的新视野号任务运营经理爱丽丝·鲍曼 (Alice Bowman) 表示，该航天器通过深空网络每周向地球报告状态信标。 “整个冬眠期间的每一份状态报告都是‘绿色’，这意味着新视野号每周都一切顺利，”她说。
鲍曼指出，随着新视野号恢复活跃运行，该团队将开始下行传输航天器的健康和安全数据，然后是来自三个科学仪器的数据。大约三周后，航天器上的爱丽丝紫外光谱仪将观察日光层外层的氢气分布，而冥王星的太阳风、冥王星高能粒子光谱仪科学调查和威尼斯伯尼学生尘埃计数器仪器将继续进行测量，地面团队将进行一系列航天器和仪器检查。
该团队还正在完成地面系统软件的升级，这将使维护航天器的运行变得更加容易。测试已经在进行中，预计将持续到今年。
新视野号正在更新的自主逻辑上运行，该逻辑专为远离太阳的操作而设计，并适应预期的功率减少和无线电信号传播时间的自然增加。
NASA 航天器对太阳系这一遥远区域的探索标志着 2006 年 1 月开始的旅程的最新一步，这是有记录以来最快的发射； 2007 年 2 月飞越木星，欣赏到这颗气态巨行星及其卫星的壮丽景色； 2015 年 7 月首次探索冥王星系统； 2019 年 1 月首次探索柯伊伯带天体 Arrokoth，此后对太阳外层日光层和数十个其他柯伊伯带天体进行了独特的研究。
有关 NASA 新视野号任务的更多信息，请访问：
https://science.nasa.gov/mission/new-horizons/

item.title: 阿耳忒弥斯二号船员和阿波罗 14 号月亮树
item.description: 在这张照片中，阿耳忒弥斯二号宇航员于 2026 年 6 月 25 日在美国宇航局约翰逊航天中心的月球接收公园参加阿波罗 14 号月球树的落成仪式。这棵树是火炬松树种的第二代阿波罗月树。最初的阿波罗月亮树是由美国宇航局宇航员斯图尔特·鲁萨（Stuart Roosa）在阿波罗 14 号上携带的种子种植的，他是前美国林务局救火员。返回地球后，林务局将这些种子发芽，并将所得的幼苗种植在美国和世界各地。

item.title: 2026 年 7 月卫星拼图
item.description:
每个月，美国宇航局地球观测站都会发布一张令人费解的卫星图像。 2026 年 7 月的益智游戏如上所示。
您的挑战识别该卫星图像中显示的位置。分享您看到的线索、您认为它在哪里以及是什么让这个地方对您来说有趣或独特。
如何回答 使用此表格提交您的回答，并选择“谜题答案”作为主题。请注明您的首选姓名或别名。
您可以保持简单，只需猜测位置即可。想要给我们留下深刻印象吗？告诉我们哪颗卫星和仪器捕获了图像，使用了哪些光谱带，或者指出有关该地区地质或历史的微妙细节。如果有什么东西引起了您的注意，或者如果这是您的家或对您来说意味着什么，我们很乐意听到。
奖品我们无法像卫星和宇航员那样提供奖金或太空之旅来观察地球。但我们可以提供几乎同样有价值的东西：益智游戏吹牛的权利。
挑战结束后大约一周，我们将在本页顶部发布答案，以及指向地球观测站每日故事图像的链接，该链接更详细地解释了该图像。我们将识别第一个正确猜测位置的人，并且我们还可能会突出显示分享特别深思熟虑或有趣答案的读者。提交回复即表示您承认您的评论可能会被编辑、摘录并发布在此页面上。
在那之前，请放大，仔细观察，并享受挑战。揭晓时见！

item.title: 250 英里之外的世界杯
item.description:
多年来，国际空间站上的宇航员拍摄了多个举办 2026 年 FIFA 世界杯的城市。
2026 年夏季，北美的 16 个体育场举办了 FIFA 世界杯的比赛。多年来，国际空间站上的宇航员从上到下拍摄了许多这些场地周围的基础设施、景观和生态系统的视图。
其中六场比赛在旧金山湾区体育场进行，比赛从 6 月 13 日开始，卡塔尔队对阵瑞士队。该体育场（也称为李维斯体育场）位于加利福尼亚州圣克拉拉，毗邻圣何塞，距旧金山以南约 40 英里（64 公里）。国际空间站上的一名宇航员于 2022 年 7 月 26 日拍摄了这张照片（上图）。
该体育场于 2014 年竣工，周围环绕着休闲、住房和商业基础设施。该场景包括旧金山湾的南部地区，该地区历经 50 年的努力，将该地区 90% 的盐池恢复为潮汐湿地和沼泽地，同时保留了部分制盐遗产，目前已投入 23 年。
7月1日，旧金山湾区举办了第六场也是最后一场世界杯比赛，美国队在淘汰赛中对阵波斯尼亚和黑塞哥维那。美国队以2-0获胜晋级16强。
国际足联世界杯决赛定于 7 月 19 日在纽约新泽西体育场举行，该体育场是新泽西州东卢瑟福梅多兰兹体育中心的一部分。该体育场（也称为大都会人寿体育场）位于曼哈顿中城以西的新泽西州收费公路沿线。请注意，北方位于这张照片的右下角，由一名宇航员于 2022 年 4 月 17 日拍摄。
该地区经历了几个世纪的人类影响。殖民者清理了湿地和雪松林作为定居点，随后进行了一系列经济和工业用途的开发。 20世纪，它成为一个不受监管的垃圾场。然而，近几十年来，湿地恢复工作与体育和娱乐综合体的发展同时进行。
其他世界杯主办城市也出现在宇航员摄影和卫星图像中。瓜达拉哈拉体育场（Estadio Akron）、洛杉矶体育场（SoFi Stadium）、休斯顿体育场（NRG Stadium/Reliant Stadium）和温哥华BC Place（BC Place）都是从上方观测到的场馆。
宇航员照片ISS067-E-202213于2022年7月26日获取，使用尼康D5数码相机，使用焦距400毫米，宇航员照片ISS067-E-18580于2022年4月17日获取，使用尼康D5数码相机，使用焦距1150毫米。它们由国际空间站机组人员地球观测设施和美国宇航局约翰逊航天中心的地球科学与遥感部门提供。这些图像是由 Expedition 67 船员拍摄的。图像经过裁剪和增强以提高对比度，并且镜头伪影已被消除。国际空间站计划支持该实验室作为国际空间站国家实验室的一部分，帮助宇航员拍摄对科学家和公众最有价值的地球照片，并将这些图像在互联网上免费提供。宇航员拍摄的其他图像可以在 NASA/JSCGateway to Astronaut Photography of Earth 上查看。 凯瑟琳·汉森的故事。
当我们探索宇宙并发现更多关于我们的家园星球时，请随时了解 NASA 的最新内容。
国际空间站上的一名宇航员惊讶地拍摄到一道划过黑暗的光雨……
这座北加州城市汇聚了城市发展、绿色空间和海上活动。
一位宇航员在前往月球途中拍摄的照片，在小说中揭示了我们的星球及其在太空中的位置……
订阅地球观测站并将地球放入您的收件箱中。
美国宇航局地球观测站每天为您带来地球的深入故事和令人惊叹的图像。
开放访问 NASA 的地球科学数据档案

item.title: NASA 为美国成立 250 周年而升空
item.description:
2026 年 7 月 4 日，美国国家博览会在华盛顿特区国家广场举行，美国宇航局局长贾里德·艾萨克曼 (Jared Isaacman) 带领他个人拥有的诺斯罗普 F-5 Tiger 飞越华盛顿特区国家广场。
250 年来，美国不断突破可能的界限。从最早的探索，到迈向月球的第一步以及塑造我们未来的任务，NASA 代表了定义我们国家的发现精神。
在美国庆祝成立 50 周年之际，《自由 250》强调了创新、勇气和科学领导力如何推动美国前进，以及 NASA 如何继续为下一代拓展前沿。

item.title: NASA 寻求业界对商业空间站第二阶段的投入
item.description:
周一，美国宇航局发布了一份征求建议书（RFP）草案，征求美国公司对其下一阶段商业空间站战略的反馈，旨在确保国际空间站近地轨道活动的无缝过渡。
“美国宇航局的审查反映了我们在整个过程中从工业界听到的情况。工业界相信它能够满足时间表，并且存在一个可行的商业市场，美国宇航局是众多客户之一，”美国宇航局局长贾里德·艾萨克曼说。 “我们致力于支持这些努力，实现使这一转变成为可能的能力，并尽我们所能确保美国在近地轨道上保持持续的人类存在。”
RFP 草案以该机构 3 月 25 日发布的信息请求为基础。根据业界的意见，NASA 将继续执行其最初计划，通过充分和公开竞争授予的基于 FAR 的合同来采购商业服务。业界表示，这种方法背后有大量的资本投资，并对 NASA 获奖后吸引额外资本投资和扩大未来市场机会的能力充满信心。
NASA 打算授予固定价格、多重奖励、无限期交付/无限数量的合同，以支持开发、认证和服务。这种方法将允许美国宇航局通过早期开发选择两个或更多承包商，然后由一个或多个承包商提供最终设计、测试、评估以及认证和服务的竞争性任务订单。
行业反馈将于 7 月 27 日星期一提交。NASA 还将于 7 月 9 日星期四在该机构位于休斯敦的约翰逊航天中心举行信息行业简报会，提供文件和期望的顶级摘要。
RFP 草案使公司有机会审查和评论未来商业空间站服务的计划收购方法，帮助塑造该机构在推进其最初的商业战略时的前进道路。该战略将通过商业合作伙伴为政府提供可靠、安全、具有成本效益的服务，使NASA能够专注于人类深空探索的下一步，同时继续利用近地轨道作为理想的训练环境和阿尔忒弥斯登月任务和未来人类探索火星的试验场。
了解有关商业空间站的更多信息：
https://www.nasa.gov/commercialspacestations

item.title: NASA 的 CAPSTONE 完成了月球技术的扩展任务测试
item.description:
Lead Writer and Communications Strategist
As NASA prepares for a sustained human presence on the Moon, missions will increasingly require spacecraft that can navigate and communicate without a direct connection to Earth.
NASA’s Cislunar Autonomous Positioning System Technology Operations and Navigation Experiment, orCAPSTONE, validated and advanced these capabilities.
Designed to test and validate technologies in lunar orbit, CAPSTONE launched in June 2022 and became the first U.S. commercial mission at the Moon. The spacecraft tested operations in three-body orbits around the Moon, using the combined gravity of Earth and the Moon to reduce the fuel needed to maintain a stable lunar path. It became the first spacecraft to fly and characterize this orbit for future exploration and science missions. Owned and operated by Advanced Space, the microwave-sized spacecraft then received a 15-month mission extension, becoming a testbed for advanced communications, networking, autonomous navigation, and software-defined satellite technologies.
Rather than launch a new satellite, NASA’s Research and Technology Mission Directorate demonstrated that CAPSTONE’s existing hardware could host new applications after launch, transforming the spacecraft into a cost-effective, flexible lunar technology demonstration platform. NASA’sSCaN(Space Communications and Navigation) Division will now use the data to demonstrate innovative networking and navigation techniques on future experiments.
“Operating multiple experiments simultaneously aboard the same spacecraft allows NASA to evaluate how these technologies perform together in a real lunar environment,” said Greg Stover, director of the Advanced Research and Technology Division within NASA’s Research and Technology Mission Directorate at NASA Headquarters in Washington. “Investments in autonomous operations and resilient communications infrastructure are essential to ensuring U.S. leadership as activity around the Moon continues to increase.”
Two experiments aboard CAPSTONE used software-defined infrastructure to advance two future mission essentials: autonomous navigation and deep space communications. The autonomous Navigation, Guidance, and Control software, or autoNGC, is designed to allow a spacecraft to determine where it is, where it is going, and how to get where it needs to be without waiting for instructions from the ground. While portions of the software had previously flown in Earth orbit, CAPSTONE marked the first time autoNGC was tested at the Moon.
“To really demonstrate that something works, you have to fly it,” said Sun Hur-Diaz, principal investigator for the autoNGC technology development project at NASA’s Goddard Space Flight Center in Greenbelt, Maryland. “The real environment is key.”
Principal Investigator for the autoNGC Project, NASA Goddard Space Flight Center
Researchers also evaluated how autoNGC performed with limited contact to Earth. While NASA’sDeep Space Networkantennas were supporting the Artemis II crewed test flight around the Moon, CAPSTONE’s communications window dropped to just a few passes per week.
Those gaps became one of the experiment’s most valuable tests. Without data from Earth, autoNGC determined CAPSTONE’s location using an onboard star tracker camera to image the Moon, Earth, and other celestial bodies. The camera-based system, known as optical navigation, at times outperformed ground-based methods for real-time onboard navigation, advancing technologies for future deep-space missions.
Alongside autonomous navigation testing, CAPSTONE also testeddelay/disruption tolerant networking(DTN), a communications architecture designed for deep space. Unlike Earth-based internet systems, deep space communications must function despite long delays and frequent signal gaps. The DTN system addresses those challenges by storing information on the spacecraft when no connection is available and automatically forwarding it once communications are restored. With these demonstrations, CAPSTONE became the first to fly the latest DTN protocols beyond Earth orbit and the first to run them in NASA’s core Flight System, an open-source framework that can be implemented on any spacecraft.
In one demonstration, engineers began transmitting data from CAPSTONE to Earth, but the connection ended before the transfer was complete. The spacecraft stored the remaining data until the next communications opportunity, and transmission resumed automatically. Every piece of data made it home.
“You can imagine an astronaut walking behind a lunar hill or descending into a crater and temporarily losing connectivity,” said Ben Anderson, a systems engineer for the Near Space Network at NASA’s Goddard Space Flight Center in Greenbelt, Maryland. “This technology allows that data to be automatically retransmitted once communications are restored.”
In addition to its primary achievements, CAPSTONE’s second life as a software-defined testing platform demonstrated

item.title: 美国宇航局韦伯发现了由宇宙碰撞形成的不寻常星系
item.description:
In new images from NASA’s James Webb Space Telescope to celebrate its fourth science anniversary, a familiar galaxy transforms into something far richer, and far more complex, than ever seen before. Webb’s unprecedented sensitivity across near- and mid-infrared wavelengths cuts through the thick lanes of dust that obscure Centaurus A’s center in visible light, showing a densely packed tapestry of individual stars and an active, everchanging galaxy. These images mark four years of better-than-anticipated performance and successful science operations for the most powerful space telescope in history.
Centaurus A is 11 million light-years away from Earth, relatively close in cosmic terms. Yet, unlike most nearby galaxies, it is very active, making it a powerful laboratory for understanding how galaxies and black holes grow and evolve together.
At its core sits a supermassive black hole actively feeding on surrounding material. As it does, the black hole launchespowerful jetsand releases enormous amounts of energy, shaping the galaxy around it. At the same time, Centaurus A bears the scars of a dramatic past: a major collision with another galaxy roughly two billion years ago. The aftermath of that merger is still visible today in its unusual structure and ongoing star formation.
Visible light observations from NASA’sHubble Space Telescopecould not reveal the central region where dust blocked the view, while NASA’s retiredSpitzer Space Telescoperevealed large scale structures in the infrared without resolving individual stars. Now, Webb brings both clarity and depth, exposing the galaxy’s inner workings star by star.
“No single telescope tells the whole story,” said Shawn Domagal-Goldman, division director, Astrophysics, NASA Headquarters in Washington. “Discoveries build over time and new observatories expand on the foundations laid by earlier missions. Webb represents the most powerful step forward yet, opening a window into wavelengths and details never before accessible. This allows astronomers to examine structures and processes that other telescopes could not see.”
Webb’s mid-infrared vision highlights the galaxy’s rich dust structures, which glow in intricate shapes that surprise and even perplex astronomers. A warped, parallelogram-like band cuts across the galaxy’s center, while wisps of material stretch outward like cosmic clouds.
An “S” shaped feature, most notable in the image from Webb’s MIRI (Mid-Infrared Instrument), is also unusual and invites questions that need further study to answer. What created this shape? How does the black hole influence it? Is it influenced by merger-induced star formation?
Many of the glowing red points in the MIRI image are dust-rich stars or stellar nurseries, where aging stars are shedding material back into space or new stars are forming. This dust is the raw ingredient for future generations of stars and planets, making it central to the ongoing life cycle of the galaxy.
With Webb’s high resolution, astronomers can now study Centaurus A star by star, even in its long-obscured central region. What looks “grainy” in the image from Webb, most obvious in the combined MIRI and NIRCam (Near-Infrared Camera) view, is actually a densely packed field of individual stars, together carrying information about the galaxy’s past.
With Webb’s view of Centaurus A, it becomes a case of galactic archaeology. Each star revealed helps to reconstruct when different events happened: when older stars first formed, when activity slowed down, a burst of star formation during the collision, and stars born from gas stirred in its aftermath. Together, they form a timeline of the galaxy’s evolution.
Webb’s capabilities go beyond imaging. By analyzing light withspectroscopy, astronomers can measure how gas moves within the galaxy.
Early findingsfrom Webb show fast-moving ionized gas flowing outward, likely driven by the black hole’s activity, and warmer molecular hydrogen in a warped rotating disk near the center. These observations help explore one of astronomy’s biggest questions: How does a black hole influence an entire galaxy?
The answer appears to be complex. The black hole can trigger star formation by compressing gas, but also limit it by pushing material away. Centaurus A offers a rare, nearby view of this cosmic interplay.
By tracing dust in never-before-seen detail, resolving millions of stars, and revealing the motion of gas near a supermassive black hole, Webb transforms Centaurus A into a vivid record of cosmic history.
The James Webb Space Telescope is the world’s premier space science observatory. Webb is solving mysteries in our solar system, looking beyond to distant worlds around other stars, and probing the mysterious structures and origins of our universe and our place in it. Webb is an international program led by NASA with its partners, ESA (European Space Agency) and CSA (Canadian Space Agency).
The following sections contain links to download this article
